title: mentha angustifolia
intro: Mentha angustifolia, also known as narrow-leaved mint or Icelandic mint, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and native to Europe. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Mentha angustifolia include its ability to alleviate nausea, reduce fever, and soothe menstrual cramps, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cognitive function. Mentha angustifolia can be prepared as a tea, oil, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies, cooking, and as a natural insect repellent.
